raw.objective: “to evaluate the effects of age, total body weight (TBW), patient origin (German/Spanish), sex, and comedication on LTG clearance.” And “to facilitate the optimization of treatments with LTG”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, SEX, origin (German or Spanish), WT, comedication with VPA, LVT, CBZ, PHB, PHT, PRM Included: WT, VPA, PHT, PHB/PRM, CBZ, IND
raw.observations: Variance of CL reduced from 47.5% to 27.5% using the covariates, of which most are comedication related.
raw.structure: 1 compartment with linear absorption and elimination
raw.equations: CL/F=theta1*WT*exp(theta2*VPA)*exp(theta3*PHT)* exp(theta4*PHBPRM)*exp(theta5*CBZ)*exp(theta6*IND)*exp(eta1) Vd/F=theta7*WT Ka=theta8 F1=1 Cobs=Cipred+eps1 VPA=comedication with valproic acid (yes =1, no=0) Although their model reports covariate VPA to be modelled as exp(-theta7*VPA), their reported value for theta7 is negative. The combination of these two would lead to VPA increasing LMT clearance, while in fact it decreases it. The only logical conclusion is that either the value of theta2 should be positive, or the minus sign should be removed from exp(-theta2*VPA). Here we went for the second option PHT=comedication with phenytoin (yes =1, no=0) PHBPRM=comedication with phenobarbital OR primidone (yes =1, no=0) CBZ=comedication with carbamazepine (yes =1, no=0) IND=comedication with 2 or more inducers (yes =1, no=0)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.028 theta2=-0.713 theta3=0.663 theta4=0.588 theta5=0.467 theta6=0.864 theta7=1.5 theta8=1.3 omega1= 0.07285 sigma1=1.25

extraction_target.title: Population pharmacokinetics of lamotrigine with data from therapeutic drug monitoring in German and Spanish patients with epilepsy.

structural_model.description: 1 compartment with linear absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL/F=theta1*WT*exp(theta2*VPA)*exp(theta3*PHT)* exp(theta4*PHBPRM)*exp(theta5*CBZ)*exp(theta6*IND)*exp(eta1) Vd/F=theta7*WT Ka=theta8 F1=1 Cobs=Cipred+eps1 VPA=comedication with valproic acid (yes =1, no=0) Although their model reports covariate VPA to be modelled as exp(-theta7*VPA), their reported value for theta7 is negative. The combination of these two would lead to VPA increasing LMT clearance, while in fact it decreases it. The only logical conclusion is that either the value of theta2 should be positive, or the minus sign should be removed from exp(-theta2*VPA). Here we went for the second option PHT=comedication with phenytoin (yes =1, no=0) PHBPRM=comedication with phenobarbital OR primidone (yes =1, no=0) CBZ=comedication with carbamazepine (yes =1, no=0) IND=comedication with 2 or more inducers (yes =1, no=0)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to evaluate the effects of age, total body weight (TBW), patient origin (German/Spanish), sex, and comedication on LTG clearance.” And “to facilitate the optimization of treatments with LTG”

covariates.raw: Tested: AGE, SEX, origin (German or Spanish), WT, comedication with VPA, LVT, CBZ, PHB, PHT, PRM Included: WT, VPA, PHT, PHB/PRM, CBZ, IND

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
